sentences.text: Hey there, Courtney.
Tony, how we doing today?
Good, how are you, man?
Doing alright!
Doing alright!
Excited, excited to be getting through the summer.
Cool.
Yeah.
Any, any travel plans?
You guys enjoying the California weather?
Yes, I would have to say so.
We are, we're taking over two properties towards the end of this month that will be in, like, Vancouver, Washington, so I'm traveling up there in just, like, yeah, week or so.
That's beautiful country up there.
Yeah.
Good for you, Tony.
Thanks.
I actually… so this week, I'm going to see a cousin get married in Cancun, Mexico, so I'm actually flying to.
Oh, nice.
Yeah, so, trying to make a little vacation out of that.
You gotta….
Yeah, yeah, you gotta.
When they do the destination wedding, you gotta tack on some extra days.
Are we waiting for anybody beyond, Courtney?
Should, will anybody else be joining, or is it just us for today?
… mid….
I see.
the calendar…
It looks like Justin was a maybe, and then Chris, I don't believe, RSVP'd, so it might just be us.
Yeah.
Alright, fair enough.
3 is company, and I'll be sure to record this, and maybe I can send the recording to the rest of the team.

Really just wanted to use today kind of a check-in on how the first half of the year is going, what the back half of the year is looking like, talk a little bit about what we're adding to the tool, what's coming, and anything else on your mind as we look towards the new year.
Anything you guys would want to be thinking about or adding to this agenda, kind of before we jump in?
I don't think so.
Yeah, nothing that comes to mind, but we'll let you know if we have any questions.
Alright, cool.
So, jumping in, just kind of taking a look at what activity looks like today in our partnership.
We've settled into a really comfortable usage pattern.
This first slide here is going to talk about how many monthly active users we have in the tool, how many weekly active users.
Those are the two I really like to pay attention to.
Those tell us a lot about what the behaviors are across the 55 market surveys.

We're seeing 100 folks log in on average every month, which… Very strong for us, just want to, you know, see how that relates and what that sounds like to you guys, based on your understanding of the team's workflows.
But, the fun graph at the bottom always kind of shows the spikes at the beginning of a week, as well, in that yellow.
But this looks really strong to us.
We like to see anywhere between 2 users per market survey is really where we like to be seeing that hang out.
Okay, cool.
This report tells us about where people are spending their time.
It's no surprise to me that most of the folks are spending their time in the market survey page.
specifically on that first market survey page, we've made it so much more powerful that a lot of the information folks need on a day-to-day lives there now.
But we also see a really cool thing.

Maybe 20% of the users are spending their time in the units tab, which is not something we often see, but is a really cool indication that folks are kind of drilling down into the details of what's going on in the market, and really getting into the nitty-gritty unit-by-unit pieces, and evaluating metrics at that level.
Does that kind of resonate with how you're hearing folks talk about the tool, or how you yourselves are using the tool?
I would say yes, I can go first, but yeah, I mean, start at the market survey, we drill into the units tab a lot.
You know, … a lot, I think, for Chris and myself and Courtney, for sure, we're the ones in there, you know, a lot.
But we're teaching teams how to look in there as well.
Yeah.
They didn't even know.
Like, I was talking to, like, I think one or two people over the last two weeks, something like that.
They didn't even know that existed, and I was like, oh yeah.

And that is definitely what I wanted to ask about, too, because I noticed that some of these tabs weren't… they're not as large as I typically see with other organizations about your size, and I wanted to maybe talk about whether folks knew about the floor plans tab, whether folks are taking advantage of the historicals tab, because from this data, it doesn't look like it all the way, but maybe that's just not how the teams are feeling like they need to use the tools.
So, that's kind of the question I had for you.
Is it a lack of awareness, or is it just not part of their workflows?
I will tell you, give you some feedback that you can take that, I mean, if you guys think about this, if you could enhance it, it'd be, I think, a big game changer.
What we're seeing, especially when we're drilling down the units tab, and this is why I was telling people, like, look into that units tab.

Is because we're trying to compare apples to apples when… if we're just looking by the comp survey, and we're seeing starting rents, and even, like, you know, net effective rent and whatnot, and we're like, whoa, why are we so… why is that comp?
Now, what we're seeing is, like, they were above us $100, and now they're down below us, like, $300, $400.
On their two bedrooms or whatnot.
But when you drill down, what a lot of people are doing, and I'm not saying we haven't done it before, but these comps are doing a loss leader.
So….
Oh, they're good.
Doing, like, one home that's $400 lower than all the others, right?
Right.
And that's drawing from the comp survey.
It looks like their market price has dropped dramatically, you know, on all their two-bedrooms, but that's not the case.
It's really that one unit that's drawing that lower rent down.
So we really shouldn't be making, you know.
vast comparisons or pricing differences or strategy based on because they want that one home that they're trying to do, but it's all good to know, you know, as well.
But if there's any way… that's… that data, like, if you go to the units tab, and I, you know, basically do filters, and I sort.
You can find it, but it does take a little bit of time.
to do that, and so if there's any way you guys can highlight that, like, where, like, I don't know, it's highlighted, hey, one unit, two units at… had dropped by $300, or whatnot, you know?
A metric, biggest drop or biggest change in price, perhaps.

Maybe, but also, like, but something that highlights, like, hey, this is only, like, one or two units, or, like, less than 10% of their available homes, or something like that, have dropped by, like, $300, or something… something that would highlight it, that would pop out, to be like, hey, that's the strategy they're doing.
To try to get those leads.
Right.
Right.
Is there a market that comes to mind where you were just maybe seeing this?
Shadow Oaks.
Yep.
Go to Shadow Oaks.
This is… I don't know if it's still there, but I was looking at Friday.
Yeah, I looked at, … you can look at… Citygate was doing it, and Sage, I think, was kind of doing it.
And look at CityKate's units, it's.
Yeah.
units.
Right.
I might sort by rent, maybe, just to make it easy.
Yeah, rent.
Yeah, some of them are a little bit lower.
Go to.
Sure.
Sage?
New bedroom… yeah, this was on Friday, so….
Okay, no.
And they're doing… and they're making… look at that one.
Oh, yeah.
It is a studio, though, but, like, look at….
It's a huge.

And Courtney, I think you've brought up something similar to me when we were talking about maybe the Excel exports and how you're using… you're having to create all these spreadsheets to maybe track some of these like-for-like or apples-to-apples.
And we rolled out the floor plans report.
I'm wondering if this is a place, now that the filters are here, if this is a place where we can maybe get at that.
It might require, you know, kind of… Jerry-rigging something here, but what if you created a minimum, not square footage, but a minimum rent value that omits Such a floor.
Hmm.
Perhaps omits the price of the, of the lowest, you know, the loss leader.
Yeah.
I've had some customers do this to some success.
It requires you, of course, to know, you know, what the deltas are.
Yeah.
where you want to be, but using some combination, maybe, of sizing, rents, and then adding your bedrooms, bathrooms here can potentially help highlight what specific floor plans are doing what.
Although this is really weird.
Why are the… okay, the floor plan names are coming through bizarre.
That's strange.
Is this typical, Tony?
name.
Yeah.
Sorry.
I do see weird floor plan names sometimes.
Okay.
That's odd to me.
Alright, I'm gonna send this to our product team later.
But okay, really looking to find… can ApartmentIQ highlight where that lost leader is?
Yeah, yeah, exactly.
Like, if that comp is doing that strategy.
Which is fine, but if… to basically be like, hey, we're aware of this, and this is what they're doing, you know?
Yeah.

And that's what I was telling, I was like, you know, talking to Regional, and I was like, we should just do that same strategy, like, combat it, like, make our, like, longest vacant just a super lost leader.
Yeah.
To….
You know, to be competitive.
Because what… they're doing it to just get the leads in the door.
Of course, right.
And I guess when you sort by days on market, maybe it becomes quite apparent.
151 days….
Okay, that's interesting, yeah, the rent trend, let's see if we move the rent trend, see what they've done in the last 3, we'll call it 3 months.
Where's that 151?
Okay, so here it's gone up, but only $5 per square foot over the last 3 months.
Interesting!
Okay.
That's great feedback, Tony.
Yeah?
And then, are the historicals a part of your workflows as well, or is this not something we're.
We….
often.
We look at it a little bit, but not, like, a ton, a ton.

The biggest thing that we try to strive, tell people to, like, regionals and whatnot, is, like, hey, why are, like, when they're like, why are they giving away 2 months or, like, a heavy concession, but they're 95%?
You know, or 5% available.
We're like… but we have to keep telling them, look, this is only what they're showing.
Right.
You know, on their website and everything.
So if there was a disclaimer like that, that would also pop out and highlight.
That, I don't know, like, to make that easily more aware.
You know.

Let's see, we did… so, we added… One of the things I wanted to highlight, and I'm sure you guys have noticed it already because you're in the tool, is the distinction between these observed metrics here at the property summary, where we're capturing all this information, and then our calculated metrics.
So, that idea… this idea of, hey, this is calculated, this is something you might want to take a look at, we've also added, in some markets where relevant, a limited data tag.
I'm wondering if we've seen that yet, if I can maybe find.
No, I don't think so, I don't know, Courtney, if you have.
Okay, because I know we have the lease subtag, which.
Yeah.
here on the far right, which we hope is just, like, an extra flag, hey, take another look here.
Okay, here's a perfect example.
Limited data.

So what we're doing now, part of our iterations on our logic to keep ApartmentIQ growing and being that tool that gets public data as close to accurate as possible to maybe a rent roll, is tweaking our logic and using analysis to understand and start to predict where we think properties are withholding units from advertisements.
Oh, okay.
And so that's what this limited data tag here is doing.
It's trying to tell you, hey, at Verdale Point, their behavior, from what we can tell on the website, is common or inconsistent with a property that may be withholding units.
So, that's here.
You can click on this, and it will show you what ApartmentIQ can read.
It's… you keep that limited data tag there to remind you, hey, take this with a grain of salt, you know, they may not be at 87% leased, actually, you know.
I have seen that, I didn't know you could click on it, though.
Yeah, yeah, yeah, and that's something that a lot of folks have missed, so I do wanna….
Nice.
that out there, and we've… Because of that, we've also moved around where our metrics live, so we want.
mature.
We're enabling all the ones we find helpful.
And you'll see when I do that, we have this new pop-up, not sure if we've seen this yet, but this is another kind of layer we're putting in.
Advertised occupancy being that projected metric, something to consider and take with a grain of salt.
I do like….
Leaning more on exposure.
Yeah.
And the reason we do that is because we're really good at… seeing what's available on a website.
And exposure, the way we do it, is just what are the available units over the units that are totaled at the property.
So, that's a really consistent and normalized number across the market survey, whereas a occupancy requires a few assumptions, right?
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
what's make ready on vacancy?
What's truly vacant versus maybe, taken down for renovation, or what have you.
For sure.

We launched pre-leased, that's a recent metric, we'll talk a little bit about it in the slideshow, but we're kind of throwing new metrics out here to try and make it really clear and hone in on the right numbers that are relevant for you.
Cool.
That's… that was a lot of, cool conversation that came from this report, so I'm gonna keep bringing this report into our, analysis here.
Be interested to see how this shifts, maybe, as we drive some awareness to the other tools and the other reports, and… and see if they become relevant or not.
Okay.
Definitely keep the feedback coming, Tony.
Yeah.
Courtney, anything from your end to add?
Not really.

I do think the loss leader… and, like, for the loss leader thing, it's more, like Tony said, it's more just about, like, strategy, and, like, we… to your point, we can figure it out, but just making it easier.
Sure.

Because sometimes, like, yeah, sometimes I think, too, managers can get caught up on, like, that lowest price, and I think if it were a little bit more obvious, they might be able to make those more accurate suggestions, too, but that's all.
If we wanted to show, maybe… would an average be more helpful to kind of look at these numbers, or is that kind of not… is that not necessarily the issue here?

Yeah, it's not necessarily… I would… I would personally say, Tony, you can tell me if you disagree, I would say it's not necessarily the average, just because you know as well as we do, like, the square footages for our one-bedrooms versus another could just be so, so off, or like….
Right.
there's just so many other factors involved, I think just being able to see the quantity would be helpful.
Yep.
When you're in the floor plans report, is it helpful To start taking a sizing range into consideration when looking at given floor plans, so that you can see the floor plans that are most like yours.

For instance, when I look at here, immediately what I'm thinking, and what maybe… when, Courtney, when you're talking apples to apples, the iron point has, two very specific floor plans in the 1x1 space.
They're both in the 780 foot square foot range.
You know, things in the 900 square foot range are probably irrelevant.
Things in the 600 square foot range are probably irrelevant.
Is it helpful to use this sizing filter, or do you think you probably wouldn't bother with this kind of tool, or this kind of slice?
I think it depends.
I know it's, like, not a straight answer.
I think it just depends.
Like, I can tell you from experience, Tony, like, I'm thinking about, like, Kent Place, or Isabella, or somewhere, like.

We know the square footages, for example, at our communities might be, like, much larger, and that definitely is a factor, but we also, like devil's advocate, we like to look at it from a prospect point of view, where they don't always look at.
Yeah.
like, for, like, square footage, right?
They're more looking at, like, one bedroom here versus one bedroom there.
And if the… the difference in square footage is vast, then that might make it, you know, play.
A factor in their decision, but we try not to focus too, too much on square footage, because we know that some prospects aren't even looking at that.
Yeah.
as closely.
Fair enough, yeah.
Yeah, yeah, I completely agree.
Chris will.
Chris will, from a pricing Because he's always like, yeah, but their square footage is this, and 50's less than us, and blah blah blah.
But from a marketing perspective.
people are going for the price, and that's what's driving them, and 50 square feet, like, it depends on how that permit's laid out.
Once they submit the location.
They might… they probably won't care about 50 square feet at all.
They like that pricing or whatnot.
That's really driving the leads from a marketing perspective that we're seeing, you know, up top, but… ….
Yeah.
So it does depend.
Yeah, I think that this is helpful in certain cases, like, the minimum.
Same square.
But, yeah.
Yeah.
Sure.

I appreciate you guys, hanging with me on the Spanish Inquisition here, just getting my couriers, and want to understand how we're using the tool, and if there's anything else as you're using the tool that comes to mind like this, feel free to give me a shout.
For sure.
Moving on, just priorities for the rest of the year.
You know, are there any big dispositions coming?
Are we changing our acquisition strategy?
Anything that you see, big roadblocks coming up that, maybe impact your use of the tool, or other tech parts of your tech stack as you're… as you're evaluating the back half of the year?
Anything coming to mind?
Hmm… Not really, I mean, we're gonna have some acquisitions and some dispositions, we're in the middle of that, so that… but that's always happening, right?
Right.
For probably every one of your clients.
And whatnot, … I don't know if there would be any other challenges….
Yeah, I don't think really anything that comes to mind.
We are in the middle of, like, an RFP process for new websites, but that's not, like, 100% happening.
Yeah, smart.
It's… we're kind of in the exploration phase space, so if that does move forward, we'll let you know, but I don't think that should have any major impact anyway.
Yeah, it would just be letting us know when the website's changed, that we can make sure there's no drop in data.
Okay, well, if it does happen, it wouldn't… be anytime, like, right now.
It'd probably be next year.
Okay, yeah, we'll talk before then.
And then, any feedback you're hearing from the team, or is there anything we can do to better support you?
I get the feeling maybe there's a chance folks don't all know about the new tools.

Is it helpful for me to maybe reach out to teams and set up a… Kind of an office hours training, here's new features, or how are we handling those kinds of communications to the teams on the ground using the tool?

I mean, I will say, like, I am bringing it up when we're talking to them, and talking to the regionals, and kind of educating them that way, and that's probably the best… Way, because right now, we're still, like, I still don't have a bunch of managers, and Courtney, correct me if I'm wrong when I'm talking to managers, there's still a bunch of managers that don't look at this once a week, even.
You know, they do, they'll say they look at it, but, like, a few times, you know, a month or whatnot.

But, and even leasing consultants, when I'm talking, I'm like, hey, use this as a tool, like a selling tool, a closing tool, like, to be helpful to your prospects, to be like, hey, I can save you all this trip of looking around, or… or wasting your time.
I have all this… live, real-time data at my fingertips.
So we are bringing this up and talking to a lot of teams about this all the time.
But I will say, I mean, and it's like our trainings, like our mass trainings, they're not as effective as one-on-ones.
Like, when we're like, hey, check this out, do this, and blah blah.
Yeah.
Because it's just, people just are like, yeah, yeah, yeah, I do it, yeah, autopilot, like, you know what I'm saying?
Versus, like, look, look what you can do, and then people also feel more free to talk and ask, like, more specific questions, things, you know?
like that, you know, the loss leader and different, you know, things that we, you know, share with you and whatnot.
So, I think we're able to get them, like, developing and get it trained on a more effective level that way, personally.
Of course.
I don't know if you feel otherwise.
Yeah, no, I feel the same way.
I mean, we can talk internally.
One thought I did have is, like, we could do, like, a refresher course, but maybe it's, like, by sign-up?
that are, like, I need… I need this kind of thing.
But we'll have to talk about that internally.
I know there's, like, a lot going on, but I do want to make sure people, like, know how to use the tool.
It's just about, like Tony said, in those MASH trainings, getting people to.
Yeah.
Yeah.
You know how it goes.
Yeah.
More managers are adopting it and looking at it more often.
There's some that still need it, but now it's more or less, like, I want the, like I was saying, I want the lease consultants to use it as a tool.
Okay.
You know.

Yeah, and I mean, of course, I'm asking, one, I want to make sure that everybody who needs this tool is… has the access and is using it, but on the other hand, I want to make sure that, you know, when you guys are thinking about the tool.
does it make sense to keep us on, right?
You know, are you guys getting the value?
Are your teams getting the value?
How important is it for you?
I know you, Courtney, or Tony, you're both using the tool really well.
Is it very important to you that the teams are using it when you kind of evaluate whether you want to keep ApartmentIQ on?
Or is that not as much of a… Driver for our partnership.
I would have to say, I mean, it's not as much of a driver, but it's like, we have this amazing tool.
We use it all the time.
Like, we're always preaching about, use all the tools that are available to you, because We get a lot of, like, excuses from some teams, like the this, and the that, and they're all.
I'm confused.
You know what I'm saying?
And we have to dispel these and be like, we totally understand, but there are… there's information and tools at your disposal that you're just not fully utilizing to overcome those, you know?
Yeah.
Gotcha.
But I would say, I will say, though, that our, like, regionals use it all the time.
They do.
Beautiful.
really, really good, I think.
I hear them talk about it all the time, and obviously, like, Chris, and I think, like, some of our executive team do, too.
So….
Excellent.
Yeah.
Excellent.
Can I make a suggestion, perhaps two thoughts I had, as we're talking about, kind of, getting these folks using the tool?
Of course, yep.
Something I've seen… I've done with organizations who feel it's really important to try and get their teams in, it sounds like we're kind of… It'd be nice if they use the tool.
We have the tool, we're paying for the tool.
Be great if they use it.
What we've done in the past is enable the SSO feature, to make it, one, to reduce the friction required to get into the tool.
I don't want to just turn that on for you guys, without clearing it with you first, but that might make it easier.
At least it reduces an obstacle of, did I lose my password?
You know, where is the website?
Blah blah blah.
The second piece is automatically scheduling some of these reports.
whether it be the Excel export or the PDF, Automatically sending that to the list of users that are associated with a given market survey.
That has shown us in the past, folks are getting this information now.
They say, oh, cool, let me learn more about this, it hits my inbox every week.
that we've seen some good gains there in user engagement.
With the SSO, we find that it reduces the, hey, I lost my password, and I just, you know, I can't be bothered to take the 10-15 minutes to go through the password reset cycle, or whatever it takes.
Would we… would we be comfortable if I enable that for the team, and maybe send a mass email that said, hey, you've got SSO enabled, just click this button, and you'll get to ApartmentIQ?
That's a good question.
I don't know, Courtney, what are your thoughts on that?
I know our teams get a lot of emails.
Already.
Yeah, well, just to clarify, we do have SSO, like, we have.
Yeah, we do.
Oh, good, okay.
use that, so everybody should already… everybody does already have that.
The email piece… I'm not sure.
Yeah, I think we would need to talk about it a little bit more before we just, like, add another.
You definitely don't want to be more noise in the inbox, that's for sure.
talk about it, and we think it'd be helpful, totally.
But, I don't know if I can just say yes right now.
Yeah, exactly.
Like, I don't really hear a lot of feedback towards logging in and issues and doing that as much.
Anymore at all.
Okay.
You know….
Cool.
Then maybe we'll try something else.
Maybe if it's just sending them, like, a, hey.
Here's a new feature in ApartmentIQ, I don't know, here's a new, here's a new report.
Alright, that's something to think about, I'll chew on that, for sure.
Yeah.
Cool.

And I like asking these questions just because the other day, for instance, we got a bunch of dispositions from one of our partners, like, 30% of the portfolio went out the door, and we said, what happened?
Oh, wow.
Oh, we've just decided to let go of our third-party revenue management arm.
And they were like, alright, why didn't you tell us?
So that was just a thing.
So I like to ask these questions, and I appreciate you guys hanging in there with me.
Totally.
Keeping an eye on the time.
Yeah.
Yeah, also, I'm so sorry, I have a hard stop, so I do have to log off, but….
Right.
Will you send this deck.
Yeah, I'll send the rest of the deck over.
Okay.
Definitely.
Don't want you to miss out, Courtney.
I also have a hard stop.
Oh, great.
Sure, that's my fault.
I always get too chatty on these.
Thanks so much for your time today, guys.
I will send this over.

The big things I wanted to highlight, we talked about a little bit already in the market survey, and you've probably already seen them, because you guys are in the tool, but on the horizon, senior housing coming, this week.
Oh, nice.
We've got a custom sort order of the comps so that you can confirm and move everything the way you want it.
when you're looking at ApartmentIQ, that we expect in September.
And then portfolio reporting, we also expect to come before the end of the quarter, so you have a group of properties, and you can get all the reporting at once on those properties.
So….
Awesome.
NASA's coming, I'll send this over to you guys.
Thank you so much for your time.
Thank you so much, Bill.
it.
Take care, guys.
Bye now.
Bye-bye.
